explanation.rule: Adding -ion (or its variants -tion, -ition) to verbs creates nouns that describe processes, actions, or results.

groups.note: Added to verbs to form nouns describing actions or results

examples.definition: an event where people try to win

wordForms.example: She made a helpful suggestion about how to improve the project.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to put forward an idea for consideration

wordForms.example: It was a difficult decision to leave her job.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make a choice about something

wordForms.example: He has an impressive collection of rare stamps.
The new extension is a welcome addition to the house.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to put something with something else

wordForms.example: The dictionary gives a clear definition of the word.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to explain the meaning of something

wordForms.definition: an event where people try to win
wordForms.example: She entered a national competition for young artists.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to try to win against others

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The committee made an unusual ___ that surprised everyone.
After days of debate, they finally reached a ___.
The museum has an outstanding ___ of modern paintings.
The ___ of the tournament attracted thousands of fans.

transformExercises.sentence: The ___ to reduce costs was approved by the board. (SUGGEST)
It was a brave ___ to quit her well-paid job. (DECIDE)
Each word entry has a clear and concise ___. (DEFINE)
